signals.reasoning: Cover the live short. The position is already underwater, the move against it looks like a short-covering rally, and liquidation distance is only about 6.6%, which is too tight to defend under an active veto regime.
Price and open interest are both rising, but the veto regime blocks chasing fresh longs and there is no clean bearish fade here either.
ETH is showing trend-supported strength with rising open interest, but under veto this is watch-only rather than a fresh long.

This is the cleanest crowded-long fade on the sheet: price is up sharply, funding is above 1 bp, open interest is expanding with the rally, and the setup looks extended enough to lean short rather than chase.
WLD is up strongly but the data quality is weaker and the spread is wide, so under veto it is better treated as watch-only.
The move already looks like a long-liquidation flush and daily liquidity is too thin to press the short aggressively after the drop.
STBL remains a valid bearish fade because funding is extremely positive, crowding is high, and the long side still looks expensive despite the veto backdrop.
Mild upside with neutral mechanics is not enough to justify either a fresh long or a fresh short here.
The rally looks more like short covering than real trend support, so it is a reasonable secondary fade, but liquidity and spread keep confidence moderate.
FOGO has already dumped hard and now reads more like a flush than a clean fresh short, so I would not press it here.
VVV is strong but extended, and under veto that makes it a watch-only name rather than a chase long.
